---
name: Bug report
about: Report incorrect behaviour. A maintainer must agree this issue before a fix PR is raised.
title: "[Bug] "
labels: ["bug"]
assignees: []
---

<!--
A maintainer must AGREE this issue, in a comment, before a fix PR is opened.
Non-trivial fixes follow the full Spec Kit lifecycle; tiny obviously-correct
fixes may skip it but still need an agreed issue.

TITLE: keep it short and scannable — "[Bug] " plus a summary a reader can take
in at a glance. All the detail belongs in the body below, not the title.
-->

## Labels

<!-- The `bug` type label is applied for you. Add the area(s) yourself — an unlabelled issue
     drops out of the backlog's filters. See CONTRIBUTING.md#labelling. -->

- [ ] At least one `area:*` label added (`area:terminal`, `area:editor`, `area:explorer`, `area:preferences`, `area:themes`, `area:ui-shell`, `area:projects`, `area:vcs`, `area:agents`, `area:extensibility`, `area:infra`, `area:platform`).

## What happened (observed outcome)

<!-- Plain language: what did you see? -->

## What you expected (intended outcome)

<!-- Plain language: what should have happened? -->

## Steps to reproduce

1.
2.
3.

## Environment

- OS / version:
- throng branch or commit:
- Elevated (admin) run? yes / no

## Evidence

<!-- Logs, screenshots, failing test output, exit codes, etc. -->

## Maintainer agreement

<!-- Leave blank — a maintainer fills this in by agreeing in a comment below. -->
- [ ] Agreed by maintainer. Contributor may proceed with a fix (full lifecycle unless trivially small).
